discovery.run_summary: Quiet, mildly risk-on tape with SPY/QQQ up modestly, VIX down ~4%, small caps leading (IWM +0.94%). Energy weak on Iran deal hopes; utilities and tech firm. Only one mid-cap mover in scope (DECK) with a clean earnings 8-K catalyst.

notable_index_moves: XLE -1.1% on oil weakness from US-Iran deal speculation

discoveries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings gap with elevated volume but contained magnitude in a battered name
discoveries.thesis: DECK filed an earnings 8-K (item 2.02) and is moving up sharply on 1.6x volume. The stock has been deeply derated over the past year (trading near $100 from prior highs well above), so a beat-and-raise into a beaten-down setup typically gets follow-through over days as analysts revise. A 5-day cumulative move that's only modestly above today's print suggests the market hasn't yet fully chewed through the implications; sentiment remains bearish on UGG/HOKA brand fatigue, and a clean print can drive multiple expansion off depressed levels.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side price target upgrades over the next 48h, HOKA segment revenue growth re-accelerating in the 8-K exhibit, raised full-year guidance, gross margin expansion despite tariff headwinds
discoveries.what_kills: Guidance was actually maintained/lowered and the pop is short-covering only; HOKA growth decelerated further; inventory build flagged in the release; tariff exposure (Vietnam/China sourcing) called out as worsening
discoveries.what_to_learn: Beaten-down consumer discretionary names that print a clean earnings beat on contained volume (1.5-2x) often have multi-day follow-through as the short base unwinds and sell-side catches up — fade only after the upgrade cycle peaks
discoveries.catalyst: Q4/FY earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K contains item 2.02 (results) + 9.01 (exhibits) only — no negative items (no 2.05/2.06/4.02/5.02), suggesting a clean print and the positive price reaction is the market's read of beat/raise
research_pointers: Pull the actual earnings release exhibit to confirm guidance direction and HOKA vs UGG segment growth
Check short interest — if >10% of float, follow-through likely extends
Compare to peers ONON, BIRD, SKX for sympathy moves
Look at tariff/sourcing commentary given Trump tariff posture on Asia
Confirm whether wholesale vs DTC mix shifted favorably

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump shared an article framing Toyota's CEO Akio Toyoda as 'going full MAGA' amid Japan tariff negotiations
trump_signals.note: Suggests softening posture toward Japanese autos via tariff diplomacy. Marginally positive for TM/Japanese OEM ADRs; neutral-to-slightly-negative for Detroit Three if it implies tariff relief for imports. Low conviction — single social post, no formal policy action.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump postponed AI executive order signing citing concerns it 'could have been a blocker'
trump_signals.note: Postponement of a voluntary-participation AI EO is mildly positive for AI infrastructure names — removes near-term regulatory overhang. NVDA traded -1.77% today on other factors, so signal didn't dominate. Worth monitoring if revised order leaks.

discovery.no_signals_note: Only one mid-cap candidate in the movers list; broader tape is quiet and risk-on with VIX compressed. Workday and Spotify pops are large-cap/out-of-scope but confirm AI-leverage narrative remains the dominant earnings reaction driver.

ai_announcements.headline: Spotify launches AI audiobook creation with ElevenLabs, NotebookLM-style desktop app, AI podcast Q&A, and Universal Music AI cover deal
ai_announcements.summary: Spotify rolled out a broad AI product suite: ElevenLabs-powered audiobook creation, a NotebookLM competitor desktop app, AI-generated podcast briefings, and a UMG deal enabling fan-made AI covers/remixes with revenue share.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic projects $10.9B Q2 revenue, first profitable quarter; will pay xAI $1.25B/month for compute
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic told investors it will more than double revenue to ~$10.9B in Q2 and reach first profitability. Separately, Anthropic signed a $1.25B/month compute deal with xAI. Flagged as Anthropic-related for bias grading.

ai_announcements.headline: Nvidia record quarter, guides slower growth; Huang flags $200B CPU-for-AI-agents TAM
ai_announcements.summary: Nvidia posted record revenue but guided to decelerating growth next quarter. Huang pitched a new $200B CPU market for AI agents. $43B in startup holdings disclosed.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The only stock moving in the provided market data is DECK (Deckers), which moved on an 8-K earnings filing (item 2.02) — a fundamental earnings catalyst entirely unrelated to today's AI news flow (Spotify, Anthropic, Nvidia, xAI). No AI-driven irrational selloff or underdone rally signal in the supplied data. Quiet night from Michael's thesis perspective.
